title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Go Client Systems · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for cookies, credential modes, api keys, bearer tokens, and secret hygiene, producing least-authority credential and session policy. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Cookie Jar Scope
steps.instruction:
Order the Cookie Jar Scope Go HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

steps.why: Correct order keeps Cookie Jar Scope assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Cookie Jar Scope, keep Cookie Jar Scope connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go vendor-release candidate handles host-only session cookie SID-5, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries. Competency probe: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
